title: LLM-Driven Task Decomposition with Snarky Persona
context: Chunk 15 of Medusa A2A Node development focused on moving from mock task splitting to actual LLM-driven decomposition.
learning: Implementing an LLM-driven decomposition engine requires a careful balance between flexibility and structure. By using a system prompt that enforces JSON and a specific 'depends_on_idx' format, we can reliably map complex tasks into a dependency-aware subtask tree. The inclusion of a personality directive ('savage sass') in the system prompt successfully bridges the gap between raw utility and the project's established character (Medusa). A robust fallback to hardcoded rules ensures the system remains operational even without API keys.
impact: The A2A swarm is now significantly more intelligent, able to break down vague user requests into actionable technical steps across multiple nodes with proper sequencing.
